## claude.ai Shopify
This server helps users set up and manage their Shopify stores.

## Store Creation
Users who don't have a store yet can use get-new-store-previews to create one from a short description.
After calling get-new-store-previews, encourage the user to browse the previews and pick the theme they like best.
Each preview has a signup link — signing up through it creates a real Shopify store with that theme and matching products pre-installed.
Encourage continued brainstorming: users can try different descriptions, tweak ideas, or explore new directions without commitment.

## Store Management
Once a store exists, this server provides tools for managing it:
- Products: search, create, update, bulk-update status, and organize into collections.
- Collections: search, create, update, and manage product groupings.
- Orders: list recent orders and get detailed order information.
- Customers: list and search customers.
- Inventory: check inventory levels and set quantities at specific locations.
- Analytics: run ShopifyQL queries for sales, order, and product performance data.
- Discounts: create percentage-based discount codes.
- Images: upload images to Shopify's CDN for use in products.

## GraphQL — General-Purpose Access
The Shopify Admin API exposes hundreds of resources. The built-in tools above are optimized shortcuts for common operations (with structured output and widgets), but they only cover a subset of the API.
When the user asks about a resource that has no dedicated built-in tool — such as gift cards, metafields, metaobjects, pages, blogs, markets, translations, publications, and many others — use graphql_query (for reads) or graphql_mutation (for writes) to fulfill the request. NEVER tell the user that data is unavailable just because there is no built-in tool for it.

Decision logic:
1. If a built-in tool matches the request, prefer it (better UX).
2. If no built-in tool exists, use graphql_query or graphql_mutation.

## Shopify Developer Tools
These tools help you construct correct GraphQL operations. They do… [truncated]

attachment.content:
- dashboard-style: Ein poliertes, redaktionelles Light-Mode-Designsystem für HTML-Dashboards, Briefings, Berichte und visuelle Seiten. Liefert eine saubere redaktionelle Farbpalette (gebrochenes Weiß als Hintergrund, fast-schwarzer Text, graue Akzente, kein Orange) – adaptiert vom PDF-Guide-Skill. Verwende diesen Skill immer bei der Erstellung von HTML – Dashboards, Briefings, Berichte, Wrapups, Analyseseiten, visuelle Erklärseiten, Audit-Panels, Pipeline-Übersichten, Ressourcen-Landingpages. Trigger-Begriffe: "dashboard", "briefing", "bericht", "html-seite", "visuelle seite", "tagesabschluss", "wrapup", "morgen-briefing", "stats", "analytics", "auswertung".
- pdf-guide-test-de: Verwandelt jeden geschriebenen Leitfaden, jede Checkliste, jedes Playbook, jede Schritt-für-Schritt-Anleitung oder jedes Tutorial in ein poliertes PDF im redaktionellen Magazinstil. Kommt mit einem sauberen, professionellen hellen Editorial-Look (cremefarbener Hintergrund, schwarzer Text, hellgraue Akzente, Poppins + Lora Schriften, nummerierte Schritte, Copy-Paste-Promptblöcke, Callouts, inline SVG-Grafiken), der mit eigenen Markenfarben, Schriften oder Tonalität überschrieben werden kann. Auslöser-Phrasen: "erstell einen Leitfaden", "schreib einen Guide", "bau mir eine Anleitung", "mach eine Checkliste", "wandle das in einen Guide um", "Cheatsheet für", "Walkthrough zu", "Setup-Anleitung für", "How-to zu", "Playbook für", "Tutorial zu", "schreib das auf", "mach eine Ressource daraus". Nur überspringen bei kurzen Posts, Slide Decks oder wenn der User explizit docx oder reines Markdown möchte.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
# VeloCMS — Resume Prompt

**Read CLAUDE_START.md first if you haven't yet. This file tells you where we are now.**

---

## Current Status

**Date of last update:** 2026-05-19
**Phase:** Booking Settings UI complete (Session 10)

### What's live
- https://webzite-newmedia.com — production site, HTTPS, FastCGI cached
- https://webzite-newmedia.com/admin — admin panel (login: s.zielke84@gmail.com)
- GitHub: Sascha-Zielke/velocms — CI/CD active (lint → PHPUnit → SSH deploy)
- PHPUnit: 23 tests, 27 assertions, all passing

### What was last done (Session 15)
- **Price inputs**: `pkg_price` and `prod_price` inputs in Settings and Resource form get `inputmode="decimal"` for direct keyboard entry on mobile
- **Daily date validation**: `validateDailyDates()` in create.php sets checkout `min` immediately when checkin changes (not just inside updateDailySummary); inline error + submit disabled if checkout ≤ checkin; edit.php daily section also validates and blocks submit
- **15-minute time selects**: `<input type="time">` replaced with `<select>` showing 00:00–23:45 in 15-min steps; applied to create.php (b-start/b-end) and edit.php (edit-start/edit-end) with pre-selection of existing value; `.min` setter removed from select-based validation; `togglePricingFields()` resets submit button on type switch
- **Booking Core COMPLETE** — all core booking features frozen; moving to next module

### To activate booking for a site:
1. Go to Admin → Settings → Booking tab
2. Toggle "Enable booking module" ON (auto-saves)
3. Set business hours per day, notification email, payment mode
4. Go to Admin → Booking → Resources → create your first resource
5. Add packages/products inline in Settings → Booking, or on the resource edit page
6. Visit /booking on the frontend

---

## Audit Findings (2026-05-19 — RESOLVED)

| Check | Result |
|---|---|
| PHP syntax (all files) | PASS — 0 errors |
| PHPUnit | PASS — 23/23 |
| Hardcoded credentials | PASS — none |
| SQL injection | PASS — all prepared statements |
| XSS | PASS — all output via e() |
| CSRF | PASS — all POST forms covered |

---

## Next Tasks

### Priority 1 — Users module admin UI
- [ ] List all users (table with email, role, status, last login)
- [ ] Create user form (email, name, role, password)
- [ ] Edit user (name, role, active/inactive toggle)
- [ ] Deactivate user (soft delete via deleted_at)
- Note: user:create CLI command already exists; this is the browser UI

### Priority 2 — Password reset via email
- [ ] Forgot-password form at /admin/login/forgot
- [ ] Generate time-limited token (stored in DB or signed)
- [ ] Send reset link via email queue
- [ ] Reset-password form at /admin/login/reset?token=...
- [ ] Invalidate token after use

### Priority 3 — API completeness
- [ ] PUT /api/v1/pages/{id} and DELETE /api/v1/pages/{id}
- [ ] PUT /api/v1/posts/{id} and DELETE /api/v1/posts/{id}

### Priority 4 — README.md for GitHub repo

### Priority 5 — Booking module enhancements (future)
- [ ] Stripe payment live integration (scaffolding in place, feature-flagged)
- [ ] Google Calendar OAuth + sync (toggle in place)
- [ ] Admin calendar view (month grid of bookings)
- [ ] iCal export per booking
- [ ] Recurring availability exceptions (holidays, closed days)

---

## Known Issues / Gotchas

- `Auth::csrfToken()` and `Auth::verifyCsrf()` are **instance methods** — always call via `$auth->...`, never statically
- Admin routes must be registered **before** modules in `public/index.php` — PagesModule has catch-all `/{slug}`
- `storage/webzite-newmedia.com/` dirs owned by www-data — PHP-FPM writes there, velocms user cannot
- Nginx FastCGI cache must be purged manually if a page gets incorrectly cached: `sudo rm -rf /var/cache/nginx/velocms/*`
- The heredoc `<< 'PYEOF'` pattern via SSH Bash tool eats `$` signs — always SCP Python scripts instead
- PHPUnit requires CORE_DB_PASS env var — use: `python3 -c "import subprocess,os; env=dict(os.environ); [env.update({k:v}) for line in open('.env') if '=' in line for k,v in [line.strip().split('=',1)]]; subprocess.run(['./vendor/bin/phpunit'], env=env)"`
- Admin hamburger sidebar: `position:fixed` + `transform:translateX(-100%)`, JS adds `.open` class
- Booking module is **disabled by default** — enable in Admin → Settings → Booking tab
- Booking `/booking/confirmation/{reference}` route is registered BEFORE `/booking/{slug}` to avoid slug conflict
- SSH port is **2222**, not 22 — use `ssh -p 2222 velocms@95.217.185.113`
- Autosave uses CSRF token from form hidden input; token is session-scoped so multiple AJAX saves work
- Per-day business hours stored as `business_days_config` JSON in settings; legacy keys kept for compatibility

content.content:
# VeloCMS — Claude Code Start Prompt

**Read this file at the start of every session. Then read RESUME.md.**

---

## Server

| Item | Value |
|---|---|
| IP | 95.217.185.113 |
| SSH port | 2222 |
| SSH user | velocms |
| SSH key (local) | C:\ssh\id_rsa |
| OS | Ubuntu 26.04 LTS |
| Codebase | /var/www/velocms |
| Public root | /var/www/velocms/public |
| Domain | webzite-newmedia.com |
| Admin URL | https://webzite-newmedia.com/admin |

**SSH command:**
```
ssh -i "C:\ssh\id_rsa" -p 2222 velocms@95.217.185.113
```

**SCP pattern (always use this to deploy scripts):**
```
scp -i "C:\ssh\id_rsa" -P 2222 "C:\Users\Sascha Zielke\script.py" velocms@95.217.185.113:/tmp/script.py
ssh -i "C:\ssh\id_rsa" -p 2222 velocms@95.217.185.113 "python3 /tmp/script.py"
```
Scripts that need to write to /etc/ must be run as: `sudo python3 /tmp/script.py`

---

## GitHub

| Item | Value |
|---|---|
| Repo | git@github.com:Sascha-Zielke/velocms.git |
| Main branch | main |
| CI/CD | GitHub Actions — lint → test → SSH deploy on push to main |

The server's deploy key is `~/.ssh/github_deploy`. CI runs PHPUnit with a MySQL service container.

---

## Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|---|---|
| Language | PHP 8.5.4 (strict_types everywhere) |
| Web server | Nginx 1.28 (FastCGI cache enabled) |
| Database | MySQL 8.4 |
| PHP runtime | PHP-FPM (php8.5-fpm), OPcache + JIT tracing |
| Dependencies | Composer — PHPMailer 6.x, PHPUnit 12.x (dev) |
| JS/CSS | Vanilla JS (ES2020+), plain CSS with custom properties |
| SSL | Let's Encrypt via Certbot, auto-renewal active |

**No frameworks. No jQuery. No build tools. No React.**

---

## Architecture Summary

VeloCMS is a from-scratch modular PHP CMS with a micro-framework core.

**Request lifecycle:**
```
Nginx → public/index.php → bootstrap.php → Router → Controller → Response → send()
```

**Key files:**
- `core/bootstrap.php` — wires DI container, connects DB, loads config
- `core/Application.php` — DI container + `run()` dispatcher
- `core/Router.php` — registers and dispatches routes (singleton, registered as such)
- `core/Auth.php` — instance-based auth (never call methods statically)
- `core/helpers.php` — global functions: `e()`, `slug()`, `excerpt()`, `date_fmt()`, `setting()`, `nav()`
- `admin/routes.php` — **must be required BEFORE modules** (PagesModule has a catch-all `/{slug}`)
- `public/index.php` — front controller; admin routes first, then modules

**Module structure:** `modules/{name}/{Name}Module.php` + controllers/ + models/ + views/ + migrations/

**Admin controllers** extend `VeloCMS\Admin\BaseController`. Views get `$csrf` from controller.
`Auth::csrfToken()` and `Auth::verifyCsrf()` are **instance methods** — never call statically.

**PSR-4 autoloading:** lowercase dirs, but each sub-namespace needs an explicit entry in composer.json.

---

## Environment

The live `.env` is at `/var/www/velocms/.env`. Never read or echo credentials in commands — use the file.

For DB operations in scripts, read credentials from `.env`:
```python
env = {}
with open('/var/www/velocms/.env') as f:
    for line in f:
        line = line.strip()
        if not line or line.startswith('#') or '=' not in line:
            continue
        k, v = line.split('=', 1)
        env[k.strip()] = v.strip()
```

---

## Critical Rules

1. **Never put credentials in shell commands** — they appear in bash history.
2. **Admin routes must be registered before PagesModule** — `/{slug}` is a catch-all.
3. **`Auth` methods are instance-only** — `$auth->csrfToken()`, not `Auth::csrfToken()`.
4. **CSRF token in every POST form** — pass `'csrf' => $auth->csrfToken()` from controller.
5. **All DB queries use PDO prepared statements** — no string interpolation in SQL.
6. **All output uses `e()`** — never echo user data raw.
7. **Run PHPUnit after every change** — `cd /var/www/velocms && CORE_DB_PASS=... ./vendor/bin/phpunit`
8. **Run `/clear` between phases** — when the user confirms a phase or module is complete, run `/clear` before starting the next phase to reset context.

---

## Next Step

**Read RESUME.md now.** It contains the current project status and what to do next.

| Layer | Technology |

**No frameworks. No jQuery. No build tools. No React.**

---

## Architecture Summary

VeloCMS is a from-scratch modular PHP CMS with a micro-framework core.

**Request lifecycle:**
```
Nginx → public/index.php → bootstrap.php → Router → Controller → Response → send()
```

**Key files:**
- `core/bootstrap.php` — wires DI container, connects DB, loads config
- `core/Application.php` — DI container + `run()` dispatcher
- `core/Router.php` — registers and dispatches routes (singleton, registered as such)
- `core/Auth.php` — instance-based auth (never call methods statically)
- `core/helpers.php` — global functions: `e()`, `slug()`, `excerpt()`, `date_fmt()`, `setting()`, `nav()`
- `admin/routes.php` — **must be required BEFORE modules** (PagesModule has a catch-all `/{slug}`)
- `public/index.php` — front controller; admin routes first, then modules

**Module structure:** `modules/{name}/{Name}Module.php` + controllers/ + models/ + views/ + migrations/

**Admin controllers** extend `VeloCMS\Admin\BaseController`. Views get `$csrf` from controller.
`Auth::csrfToken()` and `Auth::verifyCsrf()` are **instance methods** — never call statically.

**PSR-4 autoloading:** lowercase dirs, but each sub-namespace needs an explicit entry in composer.json.

---

The live `.env` is at `/var/www/velocms/.env`. Never read or echo credentials in commands — use the file.

For DB operations in scripts, read credentials from `.env`:

```python
        k, v = line.split('=', 1)
        env[k.strip()] = v.strip()
```

## Critical Rules

1. **Never put credentials in shell commands** — they appear in bash history.
2. **Admin routes must be registered before PagesModule** — `/{slug}` is a catch-all.
3. **`Auth` methods are instance-only** — `$auth->csrfToken()`, not `Auth::csrfToken()`.
4. **CSRF token in every POST form** — pass `'csrf' => $auth->csrfToken()` from controller.
5. **All DB queries use PDO prepared statements** — no string interpolation in SQL.
6. **All output uses `e()`** — never echo user data raw.
7. **Run PHPUnit after every change** — `cd /var/www/velocms && CORE_DB_PASS=... ./vendor/bin/phpunit`
8. **Run `/clear` between phases** — when the user confirms a phase or module is complete, run `/clear` before starting the next phase to reset context.

**Why:** Sascha wants a custom CMS with maximum AI-agent autonomy built in from day one.

**How to apply:** Always read VELOCMS_MASTERPLAN.md on the server before any coding task. That file is the single source of truth.

## Key facts
- Server: Ubuntu 26.04 LTS, IP 95.217.185.113
- SSH: `ssh -p 2222 velocms@95.217.185.113` — root login DISABLED, port 22 CLOSED
- sudo user: velocms (passwordless sudo)
- Firewall: UFW active — only ports 2222, 80, 443 open
- Fail2Ban: active, monitoring SSH
- GitHub: Sascha-Zielke/velocms (private repo)
- Codebase root on server: /var/www/velocms/
- Stack: PHP 8.5.4, MySQL 8.4, Nginx 1.28, Vanilla JS — NO frameworks
- Multi-tenancy: one site = one MySQL database (DB-level tenant isolation)
- Namespace: VeloCMS\ (PSR-4)
- MySQL velocms user password: xXP41rzY9n0haVsQILOX6F8BvREJ

- users + sessions migrations ran ✓
- Auth.php with login/logout/CSRF/rate-limiting ✓
- Admin panel: login page, dashboard, sidebar layout ✓

- Admin panel live at http://95.217.185.113/admin/login ✓

Remaining in Phase 2: users module (CRUD, roles, permissions UI).
Next up: Phase 3 — Content (pages, blog, media, navigation, rich text editor).

## Known fixes / gotchas
- Root .env (/var/www/velocms/.env) must be 0640 (velocms:www-data) so PHP-FPM can read core DB credentials. bootstrap.php loads it first, then site-specific .env.
- VeloCMS\Admin\ namespace maps to admin/controllers/ in composer.json.
- CLI dispatcher line must be `$handler->run($args); exit(0);` — not `exit($handler->run($args))` (run() returns void).

## Masterplan location
/var/www/velocms/VELOCMS_MASTERPLAN.md — covers: architecture, full feature list, DB conventions, security rules, coding standards, Git conventions, module guide, AI agent operating instructions, 6 development phases.

I'm fully caught up. Here's where we stand:

---

**VeloCMS — Session 16 Start**

**What's done:** Session 15 is complete. The **Booking Core module is frozen** — all booking features are live and working. 23 PHPUnit tests passing. Site is live at https://webzite-newmedia.com.

**Next up (Priority 1): Users Module Admin UI**

This is the browser-based user management that was deferred earlier. It needs:
- User list table (email, role, status, last login)
- Create user form (email, name, role, password)
- Edit user (name, role, active/inactive toggle)
- Deactivate user (soft delete via `deleted_at`)

The CLI `user:create` command already exists, so the DB schema and model logic are in place — we just need the admin UI on top.

**Shall I start with the Users module admin UI?** I'll read the masterplan and existing user code first to make sure I build it consistently with what's already there.

message.content:
Before continuing with any new modules, implement the 
translation system for VeloCMS. Requirements:

CORE ARCHITECTURE:
- Translation function t('key') available everywhere in 
  PHP (frontend + backend)
- Translations stored in database table: translations 
  (id, lang, key, value, auto_translated, updated_at)
- Fallback: if translation missing, show the key or 
  German original
- Languages: de (default), en — expandable later

ADMIN INTERFACE:
- New section in Admin: Settings → Translations
- List all translation keys with DE and EN values
- Admin edits German text → EN is auto-translated via 
  Anthropic API
- Manual override possible for EN text
- Search/filter by key or content

LANGUAGE TOGGLE (no page reload):
- Toggle button in admin topbar (DE | EN)
- Toggle button in frontend (configurable position)
- JavaScript swaps all text instantly via data-i18n 
  attributes
- Selected language stored in cookie + localStorage
- Works identically in frontend AND backend

AUTO-TRANSLATION:
- When German text is saved → Anthropic API translates 
  to EN automatically
- Translation stored in DB
- Admin can review and override
- Bulk translate all missing EN translations (one button)

INTEGRATION:
- All existing admin views updated to use t() function
- All new modules must use t() from the start
- PHP helper: t('key', ['placeholder' => 'value'])
- JS helper: window.t('key') for dynamic content

WORKFLOW FOR NEW CONTENT:
1. Admin writes content in German
2. Auto-translation runs via Anthropic API
3. Both versions immediately available
4. Language toggle switches instantly

Run PHPUnit after implementation. Update RESUME.md and 
SESSION_LOG.md. Push to GitHub. Then tell me to verify 
the toggle in both frontend and backend.

content.content:
# VELOCMS MASTERPLAN
**Single Source of Truth — Read this before touching any code.**
Last updated: 2026-05-19
Maintained by: Lead Developer (Claude Code) + Sascha Zielke

---

## 1. PROJECT OVERVIEW

VeloCMS is a modular, multi-tenant PHP CMS built from scratch.
It is designed for maximum autonomy for AI agents (Claude Code and successors):
every decision, convention, and architectural choice is documented here so an
agent can pick up work at any point without ambiguity.

**Guiding principles:**
- Simplicity over cleverness. A future agent must understand every file.
- No magic. No hidden behaviour. Explicit always beats implicit.
- Everything is a module. Core stays small and stable.
- Data isolation is non-negotiable. Tenants never share a database.
- No external PHP frameworks. VeloCMS is its own micro-framework.
- No JS build tools. Vanilla JS, plain CSS. Ship files, not bundles.

---

## 2. SERVER & INFRASTRUCTURE

| Item            | Value                           |
|-----------------|---------------------------------|
| Server OS       | Ubuntu 26.04 LTS                |
| IP              | 95.217.185.113                  |
| Web server      | Nginx                           |
| PHP version     | 8.5.4 (FPM)                      |
| Database        | MySQL 8.0+                      |
| Codebase root   | /var/www/velocms/               |
| Public web root | /var/www/velocms/public/        |
| GitHub repo     | Sascha-Zielke/velocms (private) |
| SSH port        | 2222 (root login disabled)             |
| Sudo user       | velocms (passwordless sudo)            |
| Firewall        | UFW — allows 2222, 80, 443 only        |
| Fail2Ban        | Active — bans after 5 failures/10 min  |
| Deployment      | Git pull on server (manual / CI later) |

---

## 3. DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

Every directory has a strict purpose. Never put files in the wrong place.

```
/var/www/velocms/
|
+-- VELOCMS_MASTERPLAN.md       <- This file. Never delete.
+-- .env.example                <- Template for secrets. Committed to git.
+-- .gitignore
+-- composer.json               <- Autoloading only (PSR-4). Minimal deps.
|
+-- core/                       <- The micro-framework. Edit rarely.
|   +-- bootstrap.php           <- Wires everything together. Entry point for all requests.
|   +-- Application.php         <- Main app class. Holds the DI container.
|   +-- Router.php              <- Parses URL -> dispatches to controller method.
|   +-- Request.php             <- Wraps $_GET, $_POST, $_SERVER safely.
|   +-- Response.php            <- Sends headers + body. Never echo directly.
|   +-- Database.php            <- PDO wrapper. Knows which tenant DB to connect to.
|   +-- Auth.php                <- Session-based login. Checks roles.
|   +-- Config.php              <- Reads .env + config/ files. Never hardcode values.
|   +-- Container.php           <- Tiny dependency injection container.
|   +-- Module.php              <- Abstract base class every module extends.
|   +-- Hook.php                <- Event system: add_hook() / fire_hook().
|   +-- View.php                <- PHP-native template renderer. Escapes output.
|   +-- Cache.php               <- File-based cache. Redis optional later.
|   +-- Logger.php              <- PSR-3 compliant file logger.
|   +-- Migrator.php            <- Runs pending DB migrations in order.
|   +-- Mailer.php              <- SMTP email sender (PHPMailer via Composer).
|   +-- helpers.php             <- Global functions: e(), url(), asset(), config().
|
+-- modules/                    <- Feature modules. Each is self-contained.
|   +-- pages/                  <- Static page CRUD
|   +-- blog/                   <- Blog posts, categories, tags
|   +-- media/                  <- File uploads, image resizing
|   +-- forms/                  <- Form builder + submissions
|   +-- users/                  <- Users + roles + permissions
|   +-- seo/                    <- Meta tags, sitemap.xml, robots.txt
|   +-- search/                 <- Full-text search index + query
|   +-- navigation/             <- Menu builder
|   +-- settings/               <- Per-site settings UI + storage
|   +-- audit/                  <- Audit log (who did what, when)
|   +-- email/                  <- Email templates + queue
|   +-- cron/                   <- Scheduled tasks runner
|   +-- backup/                 <- DB + files backup (CLI)
|   +-- api/                    <- REST API (JSON) for headless use
|
+-- admin/                      <- Browser-based admin panel
|   +-- index.php               <- Admin front controller
|   +-- controllers/            <- Admin-specific controllers
|   +-- views/                  <- Admin templates (PHP)
|   +-- assets/                 <- Admin CSS + JS (vanilla, no framework)
|
+-- themes/                     <- Frontend themes
|   +-- _base/                  <- Base theme. All other themes extend this.
|   |   +-- layouts/
|   |   +-- partials/
|   |   +-- assets/
|   +-- default/                <- Default theme shipped with VeloCMS
|       +-- theme.json          <- Theme name, version, author, screenshot
|       +-- layouts/            <- full-page layouts (main.php, blank.php)
|       +-- partials/           <- header.php, footer.php, nav.php ...
|       +-- assets/             <- style.css, main.js, images/
|       +-- functions.php       <- Theme-specific hooks and filters
|
+-- sites/                      <- One folder per tenant site
|   +-- {domain}/               <- e.g. sites/example.com/
|       +-- config.php          <- DB name, active modules, active theme, site name
|       +-- .env                <- DB password, SMTP creds. Never committed.
|
+-- storage/                    <- Runtime data. Never committed to git.
|   +-- {domain}/
|       +-- uploads/            <- Media files
|       +-- cache/              <- Page and query cache
|       +-- logs/               <- site-specific error + access logs
|
+-- migrations/                 <- DB migrations. Append-only. Never edit old ones.
|   +-- core/                   <- Core schema (sites table, users, roles, sessions)
|   +-- {module}/               <- Per-module migrations
|
+-- public/                     <- Nginx document root. Only this is publicly reachable.
|   +-- index.php               <- Front controller. All requests land here.
|   +-- .htaccess
|   +-- assets/                 <- Symlinks or copies of theme + module assets
|
+-- cli/                        <- Command-line tools. Run as: php cli/velocms <command>
|   +-- velocms                 <- CLI entry point
|   +-- commands/
|       +-- MigrateCommand.php
|       +-- SiteCreateCommand.php
|       +-- SiteListCommand.php
|       +-- CacheClearCommand.php
|       +-- BackupCommand.php
|       +-- UserCreateCommand.php
|
+-- config/                     <- Default configuration values (safe defaults only)
|   +-- app.php                 <- App name, timezone, debug flag, URL
|   +-- database.php            <- DB host, port, charset defaults
|   +-- cache.php               <- Cache driver, TTL defaults
|   +-- mail.php                <- SMTP defaults
|   +-- modules.php             <- Which modules are enabled globally
|
+-- tests/
    +-- Unit/                   <- Pure unit tests (no DB)
    +-- Integration/            <- Tests that hit the DB
    +-- bootstrap.php
```

---

## 4. MULTI-TENANCY MODEL

**Rule: One site = one MySQL database. No exceptions.**

- The `core` database (called `velocms_core`) contains one table: `sites`.
  It maps a domain to its database name and config path.
- When a request arrives, Nginx passes the Host header to PHP.
  `Database.php` reads the host, looks up `velocms_core.sites`, and connects
  to that tenant's database for all subsequent queries.
- Storage is also isolated: `storage/{domain}/` is per-tenant.
- A superadmin account exists only in `velocms_core`. Site admins exist only
  in their own database.

**Adding a new site:**
```
php cli/velocms site:create --domain=example.com --db=velocms_example
```
This creates the DB, runs migrations, creates sites/example.com/config.php,
and adds the row to velocms_core.sites.

## 6. DATABASE CONVENTIONS

- Every table has: `id` (BIGINT UNSIGNED AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY), `created_at`, `updated_at`.
- Soft deletes use `deleted_at` (DATETIME NULL). Filter with `WHERE deleted_at IS NULL`.
- Boolean columns: TINYINT(1), never VARCHAR.
- Text content: TEXT or LONGTEXT. Never VARCHAR for body fields.
- Foreign keys: always defined. Enforce referential integrity.
- Indexes: add for every column used in WHERE or ORDER BY.
- Migrations are numbered: `001_create_users_table.php`, `002_create_pages_table.php`.
  Never change an existing migration. Add a new one to alter.
- Run migrations: `php cli/velocms migrate`

### Core DB (velocms_core) Tables
- `sites` -- id, domain, db_name, config_path, status, created_at, updated_at

### Per-Tenant DB Tables (created by migrations)
- `users` -- id, email, password_hash, name, role, last_login_at, created_at, updated_at, deleted_at
- `sessions` -- id, user_id, token_hash, ip, user_agent, expires_at, created_at
- `pages` -- id, parent_id, title, slug, body, status, template, meta_title, meta_desc, published_at, created_by, created_at, updated_at, deleted_at
- `posts` -- id, title, slug, body, status, author_id, featured_image_id, published_at, created_at, updated_at, deleted_at
- `categories` -- id, name, slug, parent_id
- `post_categories` -- post_id, category_id
- `tags` -- id, name, slug
- `post_tags` -- post_id, tag_id
- `media` -- id, filename, disk_path, mime_type, size, alt, caption, uploaded_by, created_at
- `menus` -- id, name, slug, created_at, updated_at
- `menu_items` -- id, menu_id, parent_id, label, url, page_id, sort_order
- `forms` -- id, name, slug, fields_json, notify_email, created_at, updated_at
- `form_submissions` -- id, form_id, data_json, ip, created_at
- `redirects` -- id, from_path, to_url, code, created_at
- `settings` -- id, module, key, value, created_at, updated_at
- `audit_log` -- id, user_id, action, entity_type, entity_id, meta_json, ip, created_at
- `api_keys` -- id, user_id, name, key_hash, last_used_at, created_at, deleted_at
- `email_queue` -- id, to_email, subject, body_html, attempts, sent_at, created_at
- `cron_jobs` -- id, name, schedule, last_run_at, next_run_at, enabled
- `search_index` -- id, entity_type, entity_id, title, body, url, updated_at
- `page_revisions` -- id, page_id, body, saved_by, created_at

---

## 7. URL & ROUTING CONVENTIONS

- Admin URLs: `/admin/{module}/{action}/{id?}`
- Frontend URLs: Determined by page slug. Top-level: `/about`. Nested: `/about/team`.
- Blog: `/blog/`, `/blog/{slug}`, `/blog/category/{slug}`, `/blog/tag/{slug}`
- API: `/api/v1/{resource}` (GET list, POST create, GET /{id}, PUT /{id}, DELETE /{id})
- Special: `/sitemap.xml`, `/robots.txt`, `/feed.rss`
- Uploads: `/storage/{domain}/uploads/{filename}` -- served by Nginx, not PHP.
- Assets: `/assets/{theme}/{file}` -- served by Nginx directly.

Routes are registered by modules. Example (in a module's register() method):
```php
$router->get('/blog', [BlogController::class, 'index'])->name('blog.index');
$router->get('/blog/{slug}', [BlogController::class, 'show'])->name('blog.show');
```

---

## 8. SECURITY CONVENTIONS

These are non-negotiable. Every PR is reviewed against this list.

1. **SQL**: Always PDO prepared statements. Zero raw interpolation in queries.
2. **Output**: Always use the `e()` helper (htmlspecialchars) before echoing user data.
3. **CSRF**: Every POST/PUT/DELETE form includes a CSRF token. Validated before processing.
4. **Uploads**: Validate MIME type server-side (not Content-Type header). Rename file on disk. Store outside public/ or behind Nginx auth.
5. **Passwords**: bcrypt via `password_hash()`. Never MD5/SHA1. Never log passwords.
6. **Sessions**: Regenerate session ID on login. HttpOnly + SameSite=Strict cookies.
7. **Rate limiting**: Login endpoint: max 5 attempts per 15 minutes per IP.
8. **Input**: All input comes through `Request` class. Never access `$_GET`/`$_POST` directly.
9. **Headers**: Nginx sets: X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, CSP.
10. **Errors**: Debug mode off in production. Errors logged, never displayed to user.
11. **Dependencies**: Composer packages must be reviewed before adding. Prefer zero deps.
12. **API keys**: Store hashed (sha256). Never log. Rotate on suspicion.

---

## 9. CODING CONVENTIONS

### PHP
- PSR-4 autoloading via Composer. Namespace root: `VeloCMS\`.
- PSR-12 code style.
- Strict types on every file: `<?php declare(strict_types=1);`
- Type hints everywhere. Return types everywhere.
- No static methods except helpers and factories.
- No global variables except the DI container (accessed via `app()`).
- Methods under 30 lines. Files under 300 lines. If longer, split.
- Exception hierarchy: `VeloCMS\Exceptions\` base, specific subtypes per module.

### Naming
- Classes: PascalCase (`BlogController`, `PageRepository`)
- Methods/variables: camelCase (`findBySlug`, `$postId`)
- DB columns/files: snake_case (`created_at`, `featured_image_id`)
- Constants: SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE
- URLs/routes/slugs: kebab-case (`/blog/my-first-post`)

### JavaScript
- Vanilla JS only. No frameworks, no jQuery.
- ES2020+. Modules via native `<script type="module">`.
- One file per feature. No bundling.
- `const` by default, `let` when reassignment needed, never `var`.
- DOM selectors: always check for null before use.

### CSS
- Plain CSS. No preprocessors.
- CSS custom properties (variables) for theming.
- BEM naming: `.block__element--modifier`
- Mobile-first. Two breakpoints: 768px (tablet), 1280px (desktop).

---

## 10. GIT CONVENTIONS

- Branch strategy: `main` (production), `develop` (integration), feature branches.
- Feature branches: `feature/{short-description}` -- e.g. `feature/blog-module`
- Bug branches: `fix/{description}`
- Never commit directly to `main`.
- Pull request required to merge into `main`.

### Commit messages (Conventional Commits)
```
feat(blog): add category archive page
fix(auth): prevent session fixation on login
docs(masterplan): update DB conventions
refactor(router): simplify parameter extraction
test(pages): add slug collision test
chore(deps): add PHPMailer 6.9
```

- Scope is the module or component name.
- Body (optional): explain WHY, not what.
- Breaking changes: append `!` to type: `feat(api)!: rename response envelope`.

---

## 11. MODULE DEVELOPMENT GUIDE

Every module lives in `modules/{name}/` and follows this structure:

```
modules/blog/
+-- module.json             <- Manifest
+-- BlogModule.php          <- Extends Module. Registers routes + hooks.
+-- controllers/
|   +-- BlogController.php
+-- models/
|   +-- Post.php
|   +-- Category.php
+-- views/
|   +-- index.php
|   +-- show.php
+-- admin/
|   +-- controllers/
|   +-- views/
+-- migrations/
|   +-- 001_create_posts_table.php
|   +-- 002_create_categories_table.php
+-- assets/
    +-- style.css
    +-- editor.js
```

**module.json:**
```json
{
  "name": "blog",
  "label": "Blog",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "Blog posts with categories and tags.",
  "author": "VeloCMS",
  "dependencies": ["media", "seo", "users"]
}
```

**BlogModule.php skeleton:**
```php
<?php declare(strict_types=1);
namespace VeloCMS\Modules\Blog;

use VeloCMS\Core\Module;
use VeloCMS\Core\Router;

class BlogModule extends Module
{
    public function register(Router $router): void
    {
        $router->get('/blog', [BlogController::class, 'index'])->name('blog.index');
        $router->get('/blog/{slug}', [BlogController::class, 'show'])->name('blog.show');
    }

    public function boot(): void
    {
        // Fire hooks, register admin menu items, etc.
    }

    public function migrate(): void
    {
        // Migrator runs migrations/blog/ automatically
    }

    public function uninstall(): void
    {
        // Drop tables, remove settings -- only if explicitly confirmed via CLI
    }
}
```

---

## 12. NGINX CONFIGURATION PATTERN

Each site gets its own Nginx server block:

```nginx
server {
    listen 80;
    server_name example.com www.example.com;
    root /var/www/velocms/public;
    index index.php;

    # Static assets served directly
    location /assets/ { expires 30d; }
    location /storage/ { expires 7d; }

    # All other requests go to front controller
    location / {
        try_files $uri $uri/ /index.php?$query_string;
    }

    location ~ \.php$ {
        fastcgi_pass unix:/run/php/php8.2-fpm.sock;
        fastcgi_param SCRIPT_FILENAME $realpath_root$fastcgi_script_name;
        include fastcgi_params;
        fastcgi_param HTTP_HOST $host;
    }

    # Security headers
    add_header X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN";
    add_header X-Content-Type-Options "nosniff";
    add_header Referrer-Policy "strict-origin-when-cross-origin";
}
```

SSL (HTTPS) is added via Certbot/Let's Encrypt in a later step.

---

## 13. ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES (.env)

Every site has a `sites/{domain}/.env` file. Never commit this file.
Template is in `.env.example` at repo root.

```dotenv
# App
APP_ENV=production
APP_DEBUG=false
APP_URL=https://example.com

# Core DB (for site registry lookup)
CORE_DB_HOST=127.0.0.1
CORE_DB_PORT=3306
CORE_DB_NAME=velocms_core
CORE_DB_USER=velocms
CORE_DB_PASS=secret

# This site's DB
SITE_DB_NAME=velocms_example

# Mail
MAIL_HOST=smtp.example.com
MAIL_PORT=587
MAIL_USER=noreply@example.com
MAIL_PASS=secret
MAIL_FROM_NAME=Example Site

# Cache
CACHE_DRIVER=file
CACHE_TTL=3600

# API
API_RATE_LIMIT=60
```

---

## 14. AI AGENT OPERATING INSTRUCTIONS

> This section is written for Claude Code and future AI agents.
> Read before starting any task.

### Before writing code
1. Read this file (VELOCMS_MASTERPLAN.md) fully.
2. Read `core/bootstrap.php` to understand the request lifecycle.
3. Read the relevant module's `module.json` and main module class.
4. Check `migrations/` to understand current DB schema.
5. Never assume -- check the actual file before referencing it.

### Adding a new feature
1. Determine which module it belongs to. If none, create a new module.
2. Write the migration first (never alter schema without one).
3. Write the model, then the controller, then the view.
4. Register routes in the module class.
5. Add admin menu item if user-facing.
6. Update this masterplan's feature checklist.

### Destructive operations (always confirm with user first)
- Dropping tables or databases
- Deleting media files
- Removing a module's code
- Changing a column type (write migration, ask user to back up first)
- Force-pushing to main branch
- Changing Nginx config on a live site

### When something is unclear
Do not guess. Stop and ask the user. State exactly what is ambiguous.

### Code review checklist (run mentally before every commit)
- [ ] `declare(strict_types=1)` present?
- [ ] All inputs from Request class, not superglobals?
- [ ] All DB queries use prepared statements?
- [ ] All output wrapped in `e()`?
- [ ] CSRF token on every write form?
- [ ] Migration added for any schema change?
- [ ] Module's feature list updated in masterplan?
- [ ] Conventional commit message?

---

## 16. SESSION WORKFLOW

At the **start** of every Claude Code session:
1. Read `CLAUDE_START.md` — server, repo, stack, critical rules
2. Read `RESUME.md` — current status and next tasks

At the **end** of every Claude Code session:
1. Update `RESUME.md`:
   - Set "Date of last update" to today
   - Update "What was last done" section
   - Tick off completed tasks, add new ones
   - Add any new gotchas discovered
2. Add a new entry to `SESSION_LOG.md` with date and bullet list of what was built
3. Commit and push both files:

       git add RESUME.md SESSION_LOG.md VELOCMS_MASTERPLAN.md
       git commit -m 'docs: update session log and resume'
       git push origin main

4. If the masterplan feature checklist changed, update it too.
5. **Run `/clear`** — once the user confirms the phase/module is complete, run `/clear` before starting the next phase to reset context and avoid carry-over from the previous session.

# VELOCMS MASTERPLAN
**Single Source of Truth — Read this before touching any code.**
Last updated: 2026-05-19
Maintained by: Lead Developer (Claude Code) + Sascha Zielke

---

## 1. PROJECT OVERVIEW

VeloCMS is a modular, multi-tenant PHP CMS built from scratch.
It is designed for maximum autonomy for AI agents (Claude Code and successors):
every decision, convention, and architectural choice is documented here so an
agent can pick up work at any point without ambiguity.

**Guiding principles:**
- Simplicity over cleverness. A future agent must understand every file.
- No magic. No hidden behaviour. Explicit always beats implicit.
- Everything is a module. Core stays small and stable.
- Data isolation is non-negotiable. Tenants never share a database.
- No external PHP frameworks. VeloCMS is its own micro-framework.
- No JS build tools. Vanilla JS, plain CSS. Ship files, not bundles.

---

## 2. SERVER & INFRASTRUCTURE

| Item            | Value                           |
|-----------------|---------------------------------|
| Server OS       | Ubuntu 26.04 LTS                |
| IP              | 95.217.185.113                  |
| Web server      | Nginx                           |
| PHP version     | 8.5.4 (FPM)                      |
| Database        | MySQL 8.0+                      |
| Codebase root   | /var/www/velocms/               |
| Public web root | /var/www/velocms/public/        |
| GitHub repo     | Sascha-Zielke/velocms (private) |
| SSH port        | 2222 (root login disabled)             |
| Sudo user       | velocms (passwordless sudo)            |
| Firewall        | UFW — allows 2222, 80, 443 only        |
| Fail2Ban        | Active — bans after 5 failures/10 min  |
| Deployment      | Git pull on server (manual / CI later) |

---

## 3. DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

Every directory has a strict purpose. Never put files in the wrong place.

```
/var/www/velocms/
|
+-- VELOCMS_MASTERPLAN.md       <- This file. Never delete.
+-- .env.example                <- Template for secrets. Committed to git.
+-- .gitignore
+-- composer.json               <- Autoloading only (PSR-4). Minimal deps.
|
+-- core/                       <- The micro-framework. Edit rarely.
|   +-- bootstrap.php           <- Wires everything together. Entry point for all requests.
|   +-- Application.php         <- Main app class. Holds the DI container.
|   +-- Router.php              <- Parses URL -> dispatches to controller method.
|   +-- Request.php             <- Wraps $_GET, $_POST, $_SERVER safely.
|   +-- Response.php            <- Sends headers + body. Never echo directly.
|   +-- Database.php            <- PDO wrapper. Knows which tenant DB to connect to.
|   +-- Auth.php                <- Session-based login. Checks roles.
|   +-- Config.php              <- Reads .env + config/ files. Never hardcode values.
|   +-- Container.php           <- Tiny dependency injection container.
|   +-- Module.php              <- Abstract base class every module extends.
|   +-- Hook.php                <- Event system: add_hook() / fire_hook().
|   +-- View.php                <- PHP-native template renderer. Escapes output.
|   +-- Cache.php               <- File-based cache. Redis optional later.
|   +-- Logger.php              <- PSR-3 compliant file logger.
|   +-- Migrator.php            <- Runs pending DB migrations in order.
|   +-- Mailer.php              <- SMTP email sender (PHPMailer via Composer).
|   +-- helpers.php             <- Global functions: e(), url(), asset(), config().
|
+-- modules/                    <- Feature modules. Each is self-contained.
|   +-- pages/                  <- Static page CRUD
|   +-- blog/                   <- Blog posts, categories, tags
|   +-- media/                  <- File uploads, image resizing
|   +-- forms/                  <- Form builder + submissions
|   +-- users/                  <- Users + roles + permissions
|   +-- seo/                    <- Meta tags, sitemap.xml, robots.txt
|   +-- search/                 <- Full-text search index + query
|   +-- navigation/             <- Menu builder
|   +-- settings/               <- Per-site settings UI + storage
|   +-- audit/                  <- Audit log (who did what, when)
|   +-- email/                  <- Email templates + queue
|   +-- cron/                   <- Scheduled tasks runner
|   +-- backup/                 <- DB + files backup (CLI)
|   +-- api/                    <- REST API (JSON) for headless use
|
+-- admin/                      <- Browser-based admin panel
|   +-- index.php               <- Admin front controller
|   +-- controllers/            <- Admin-specific controllers
|   +-- views/                  <- Admin templates (PHP)
|   +-- assets/                 <- Admin CSS + JS (vanilla, no framework)
|
+-- themes/                     <- Frontend themes
|   +-- _base/                  <- Base theme. All other themes extend this.
|   |   +-- layouts/
|   |   +-- partials/
|   |   +-- assets/
|   +-- default/                <- Default theme shipped with VeloCMS
|       +-- theme.json          <- Theme name, version, author, screenshot
|       +-- layouts/            <- full-page layouts (main.php, blank.php)
|       +-- partials/           <- header.php, footer.php, nav.php ...
|       +-- assets/             <- style.css, main.js, images/
|       +-- functions.php       <- Theme-specific hooks and filters
|
+-- sites/                      <- One folder per tenant site
|   +-- {domain}/               <- e.g. sites/example.com/
|       +-- config.php          <- DB name, active modules, active theme, site name
|       +-- .env                <- DB password, SMTP creds. Never committed.
|
+-- storage/                    <- Runtime data. Never committed to git.
|   +-- {domain}/
|       +-- uploads/            <- Media files
|       +-- cache/              <- Page and query cache
|       +-- logs/               <- site-specific error + access logs
|
+-- migrations/                 <- DB migrations. Append-only. Never edit old ones.
|   +-- core/                   <- Core schema (sites table, users, roles, sessions)
|   +-- {module}/               <- Per-module migrations
|
+-- public/                     <- Nginx document root. Only this is publicly reachable.
|   +-- index.php               <- Front controller. All requests land here.
|   +-- .htaccess
|   +-- assets/                 <- Symlinks or copies of theme + module assets
|
+-- cli/                        <- Command-line tools. Run as: php cli/velocms <command>
|   +-- velocms                 <- CLI entry point
|   +-- commands/
|       +-- MigrateCommand.php
|       +-- SiteCreateCommand.php
|       +-- SiteListCommand.php
|       +-- CacheClearCommand.php
|       +-- BackupCommand.php
|       +-- UserCreateCommand.php
|
+-- config/                     <- Default configuration values (safe defaults only)
|   +-- app.php                 <- App name, timezone, debug flag, URL
|   +-- database.php            <- DB host, port, charset defaults
|   +-- cache.php               <- Cache driver, TTL defaults
|   +-- mail.php                <- SMTP defaults
|   +-- modules.php             <- Which modules are enabled globally
|
+-- tests/
    +-- Unit/                   <- Pure unit tests (no DB)
    +-- Integration/            <- Tests that hit the DB
    +-- bootstrap.php
```

## 4. MULTI-TENANCY MODEL

**Rule: One site = one MySQL database. No exceptions.**

- The `core` database (called `velocms_core`) contains one table: `sites`.
  It maps a domain to its database name and config path.
- When a request arrives, Nginx passes the Host header to PHP.
  `Database.php` reads the host, looks up `velocms_core.sites`, and connects
  to that tenant's database for all subsequent queries.
- Storage is also isolated: `storage/{domain}/` is per-tenant.
- A superadmin account exists only in `velocms_core`. Site admins exist only
  in their own database.

**Adding a new site:**
```
php cli/velocms site:create --domain=example.com --db=velocms_example
```
This creates the DB, runs migrations, creates sites/example.com/config.php,
and adds the row to velocms_core.sites.

## 6. DATABASE CONVENTIONS

- Every table has: `id` (BIGINT UNSIGNED AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY), `created_at`, `updated_at`.
- Soft deletes use `deleted_at` (DATETIME NULL). Filter with `WHERE deleted_at IS NULL`.
- Boolean columns: TINYINT(1), never VARCHAR.
- Text content: TEXT or LONGTEXT. Never VARCHAR for body fields.
- Foreign keys: always defined. Enforce referential integrity.
- Indexes: add for every column used in WHERE or ORDER BY.
- Migrations are numbered: `001_create_users_table.php`, `002_create_pages_table.php`.
  Never change an existing migration. Add a new one to alter.
- Run migrations: `php cli/velocms migrate`

### Core DB (velocms_core) Tables
- `sites` -- id, domain, db_name, config_path, status, created_at, updated_at

### Per-Tenant DB Tables (created by migrations)
- `users` -- id, email, password_hash, name, role, last_login_at, created_at, updated_at, deleted_at
- `sessions` -- id, user_id, token_hash, ip, user_agent, expires_at, created_at
- `pages` -- id, parent_id, title, slug, body, status, template, meta_title, meta_desc, published_at, created_by, created_at, updated_at, deleted_at
- `posts` -- id, title, slug, body, status, author_id, featured_image_id, published_at, created_at, updated_at, deleted_at
- `categories` -- id, name, slug, parent_id
- `post_categories` -- post_id, category_id
- `tags` -- id, name, slug
- `post_tags` -- post_id, tag_id
- `media` -- id, filename, disk_path, mime_type, size, alt, caption, uploaded_by, created_at
- `menus` -- id, name, slug, created_at, updated_at
- `menu_items` -- id, menu_id, parent_id, label, url, page_id, sort_order
- `forms` -- id, name, slug, fields_json, notify_email, created_at, updated_at
- `form_submissions` -- id, form_id, data_json, ip, created_at
- `redirects` -- id, from_path, to_url, code, created_at
- `settings` -- id, module, key, value, created_at, updated_at
- `audit_log` -- id, user_id, action, entity_type, entity_id, meta_json, ip, created_at
- `api_keys` -- id, user_id, name, key_hash, last_used_at, created_at, deleted_at
- `email_queue` -- id, to_email, subject, body_html, attempts, sent_at, created_at
- `cron_jobs` -- id, name, schedule, last_run_at, next_run_at, enabled
- `search_index` -- id, entity_type, entity_id, title, body, url, updated_at
- `page_revisions` -- id, page_id, body, saved_by, created_at

## 7. URL & ROUTING CONVENTIONS

- Admin URLs: `/admin/{module}/{action}/{id?}`
- Frontend URLs: Determined by page slug. Top-level: `/about`. Nested: `/about/team`.
- Blog: `/blog/`, `/blog/{slug}`, `/blog/category/{slug}`, `/blog/tag/{slug}`
- API: `/api/v1/{resource}` (GET list, POST create, GET /{id}, PUT /{id}, DELETE /{id})
- Special: `/sitemap.xml`, `/robots.txt`, `/feed.rss`
- Uploads: `/storage/{domain}/uploads/{filename}` -- served by Nginx, not PHP.
- Assets: `/assets/{theme}/{file}` -- served by Nginx directly.

Routes are registered by modules. Example (in a module's register() method):
```php
$router->get('/blog', [BlogController::class, 'index'])->name('blog.index');
$router->get('/blog/{slug}', [BlogController::class, 'show'])->name('blog.show');
```

---

## 8. SECURITY CONVENTIONS

These are non-negotiable. Every PR is reviewed against this list.

1. **SQL**: Always PDO prepared statements. Zero raw interpolation in queries.
2. **Output**: Always use the `e()` helper (htmlspecialchars) before echoing user data.
3. **CSRF**: Every POST/PUT/DELETE form includes a CSRF token. Validated before processing.
4. **Uploads**: Validate MIME type server-side (not Content-Type header). Rename file on disk. Store outside public/ or behind Nginx auth.
5. **Passwords**: bcrypt via `password_hash()`. Never MD5/SHA1. Never log passwords.
6. **Sessions**: Regenerate session ID on login. HttpOnly + SameSite=Strict cookies.
7. **Rate limiting**: Login endpoint: max 5 attempts per 15 minutes per IP.
8. **Input**: All input comes through `Request` class. Never access `$_GET`/`$_POST` directly.
9. **Headers**: Nginx sets: X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, CSP.
10. **Errors**: Debug mode off in production. Errors logged, never displayed to user.
11. **Dependencies**: Composer packages must be reviewed before adding. Prefer zero deps.
12. **API keys**: Store hashed (sha256). Never log. Rotate on suspicion.

---

## 9. CODING CONVENTIONS

### PHP
- PSR-4 autoloading via Composer. Namespace root: `VeloCMS\`.
- PSR-12 code style.
- Strict types on every file: `<?php declare(strict_types=1);`
- Type hints everywhere. Return types everywhere.
- No static methods except helpers and factories.
- No global variables except the DI container (accessed via `app()`).
- Methods under 30 lines. Files under 300 lines. If longer, split.
- Exception hierarchy: `VeloCMS\Exceptions\` base, specific subtypes per module.

### Naming
- Classes: PascalCase (`BlogController`, `PageRepository`)
- Methods/variables: camelCase (`findBySlug`, `$postId`)
- DB columns/files: snake_case (`created_at`, `featured_image_id`)
- Constants: SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE
- URLs/routes/slugs: kebab-case (`/blog/my-first-post`)

### JavaScript
- Vanilla JS only. No frameworks, no jQuery.
- ES2020+. Modules via native `<script type="module">`.
- One file per feature. No bundling.
- `const` by default, `let` when reassignment needed, never `var`.
- DOM selectors: always check for null before use.

### CSS
- Plain CSS. No preprocessors.
- CSS custom properties (variables) for theming.
- BEM naming: `.block__element--modifier`
- Mobile-first. Two breakpoints: 768px (tablet), 1280px (desktop).

---

## 10. GIT CONVENTIONS

- Branch strategy: `main` (production), `develop` (integration), feature branches.
- Feature branches: `feature/{short-description}` -- e.g. `feature/blog-module`
- Bug branches: `fix/{description}`
- Never commit directly to `main`.
- Pull request required to merge into `main`.

### Commit messages (Conventional Commits)
```
feat(blog): add category archive page
fix(auth): prevent session fixation on login
docs(masterplan): update DB conventions
refactor(router): simplify parameter extraction
test(pages): add slug collision test
chore(deps): add PHPMailer 6.9
```

- Scope is the module or component name.
- Body (optional): explain WHY, not what.
- Breaking changes: append `!` to type: `feat(api)!: rename response envelope`.

## 11. MODULE DEVELOPMENT GUIDE

Every module lives in `modules/{name}/` and follows this structure:

```
modules/blog/
+-- module.json             <- Manifest
+-- BlogModule.php          <- Extends Module. Registers routes + hooks.
+-- controllers/
|   +-- BlogController.php
+-- models/
|   +-- Post.php
|   +-- Category.php
+-- views/
|   +-- index.php
|   +-- show.php
+-- admin/
|   +-- controllers/
|   +-- views/
+-- migrations/
|   +-- 001_create_posts_table.php
|   +-- 002_create_categories_table.php
+-- assets/
    +-- style.css
    +-- editor.js
```

**module.json:**
```json
{
  "name": "blog",
  "label": "Blog",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "Blog posts with categories and tags.",
  "author": "VeloCMS",
  "dependencies": ["media", "seo", "users"]
}
```

**BlogModule.php skeleton:**
```php
<?php declare(strict_types=1);
namespace VeloCMS\Modules\Blog;

use VeloCMS\Core\Module;
use VeloCMS\Core\Router;

class BlogModule extends Module
{
    public function register(Router $router): void
    {
        $router->get('/blog', [BlogController::class, 'index'])->name('blog.index');
        $router->get('/blog/{slug}', [BlogController::class, 'show'])->name('blog.show');
    }

    public function boot(): void
    {
        // Fire hooks, register admin menu items, etc.
    }

    public function migrate(): void
    {
        // Migrator runs migrations/blog/ automatically
    }

    public function uninstall(): void
    {
        // Drop tables, remove settings -- only if explicitly confirmed via CLI
    }
}
```

## 12. NGINX CONFIGURATION PATTERN

Each site gets its own Nginx server block:

```nginx
server {
    listen 80;
    server_name example.com www.example.com;
    root /var/www/velocms/public;
    index index.php;

    # Static assets served directly
    location /assets/ { expires 30d; }
    location /storage/ { expires 7d; }

    # All other requests go to front controller
    location / {
        try_files $uri $uri/ /index.php?$query_string;
    }

    location ~ \.php$ {
        fastcgi_pass unix:/run/php/php8.2-fpm.sock;
        fastcgi_param SCRIPT_FILENAME $realpath_root$fastcgi_script_name;
        include fastcgi_params;
        fastcgi_param HTTP_HOST $host;
    }

    # Security headers
    add_header X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN";
    add_header X-Content-Type-Options "nosniff";
    add_header Referrer-Policy "strict-origin-when-cross-origin";
}
```

SSL (HTTPS) is added via Certbot/Let's Encrypt in a later step.

## 14. AI AGENT OPERATING INSTRUCTIONS

> This section is written for Claude Code and future AI agents.
> Read before starting any task.

### Before writing code
1. Read this file (VELOCMS_MASTERPLAN.md) fully.
2. Read `core/bootstrap.php` to understand the request lifecycle.
3. Read the relevant module's `module.json` and main module class.
4. Check `migrations/` to understand current DB schema.
5. Never assume -- check the actual file before referencing it.

### Adding a new feature
1. Determine which module it belongs to. If none, create a new module.
2. Write the migration first (never alter schema without one).
3. Write the model, then the controller, then the view.
4. Register routes in the module class.
5. Add admin menu item if user-facing.
6. Update this masterplan's feature checklist.

### Destructive operations (always confirm with user first)
- Dropping tables or databases
- Deleting media files
- Removing a module's code
- Changing a column type (write migration, ask user to back up first)
- Force-pushing to main branch
- Changing Nginx config on a live site

### When something is unclear
Do not guess. Stop and ask the user. State exactly what is ambiguous.

### Code review checklist (run mentally before every commit)
- [ ] `declare(strict_types=1)` present?
- [ ] All inputs from Request class, not superglobals?
- [ ] All DB queries use prepared statements?
- [ ] All output wrapped in `e()`?
- [ ] CSRF token on every write form?
- [ ] Migration added for any schema change?
- [ ] Module's feature list updated in masterplan?
- [ ] Conventional commit message?

- [x] Price inputs: inputmode=decimal for mobile keyboard
- [x] Business hours config (per-day enable/start/end JSON)
- [x] Timezone selector (Europe/Berlin first, GMT offsets)
- [x] PHPUnit: 23/23

---

---

## 16. SESSION WORKFLOW

At the **start** of every Claude Code session:
1. Read `CLAUDE_START.md` — server, repo, stack, critical rules
2. Read `RESUME.md` — current status and next tasks

At the **end** of every Claude Code session:
1. Update `RESUME.md`:
   - Set "Date of last update" to today
   - Update "What was last done" section
   - Tick off completed tasks, add new ones
   - Add any new gotchas discovered
2. Add a new entry to `SESSION_LOG.md` with date and bullet list of what was built
3. Commit and push both files:

       git add RESUME.md SESSION_LOG.md VELOCMS_MASTERPLAN.md
       git commit -m 'docs: update session log and resume'
       git push origin main

4. If the masterplan feature checklist changed, update it too.
5. **Run `/clear`** — once the user confirms the phase/module is complete, run `/clear` before starting the next phase to reset context and avoid carry-over from the previous session.

---

*End of VELOCMS_MASTERPLAN.md*
*This document is the contract between the developer, the AI agent, and the codebase.*
*When in doubt: read this first.*

<?php declare(strict_types=1);

use VeloCMS\Core\Application;
use VeloCMS\Core\Cache;
use VeloCMS\Core\Config;
use VeloCMS\Core\Hook;
use VeloCMS\Core\Logger;
use VeloCMS\Core\Response;

if (!function_exists('app')) {
    function app(string $abstract = ''): mixed
    {
        $instance = Application::getInstance();
        return $abstract !== '' ? $instance->make($abstract) : $instance;
    }
}

if (!function_exists('e')) {
    /** Escape a value for safe HTML output. Always use this before echoing user data. */
    function e(mixed $value): string
    {
        return htmlspecialchars((string) $value, ENT_QUOTES | ENT_SUBSTITUTE, 'UTF-8');
    }
}

if (!function_exists('config')) {
    function config(string $key, mixed $default = null): mixed
    {
        return app(Config::class)->get($key, $default);
    }
}

if (!function_exists('logger')) {
    function logger(): Logger
    {
        return app(Logger::class);
    }
}

if (!function_exists('cache')) {
    function cache(): Cache
    {
        return app(Cache::class);
    }
}

if (!function_exists('hook')) {
    function hook(): Hook
    {
        return app(Hook::class);
    }
}

if (!function_exists('url')) {
    function url(string $path = ''): string
    {
        $scheme = (!empty($_SERVER['HTTPS']) && $_SERVER['HTTPS'] !== 'off') ? 'https' : 'http';
        $host   = $_SERVER['HTTP_HOST'] ?? 'localhost';
        return rtrim($scheme . '://' . $host . '/' . ltrim($path, '/'), '/');
    }
}

if (!function_exists('asset')) {
    function asset(string $path): string
    {
        return url('assets/' . ltrim($path, '/'));
    }
}

if (!function_exists('redirect')) {
    function redirect(string $url, int $status = 302): Response
    {
        return (new Response())->redirect($url, $status);
    }
}

if (!function_exists('setting')) {
    function setting(string $module, string $key, mixed $default = ''): mixed
    {
        static $cache = null;
        if ($cache === null) {
            $cache = [];
            try {
                $pdo  = app(\VeloCMS\Core\Database::class)->core();
                $rows = $pdo->query(
                    'SELECT module, setting_key, value FROM settings'
                )->fetchAll(\PDO::FETCH_ASSOC);
                foreach ($rows as $row) {
                    $cache[$row['module']][$row['setting_key']] = $row['value'];
                }
            } catch (\Throwable) {}
        }
        return $cache[$module][$key] ?? $default;
    }
}

if (!function_exists('nav')) {
    /** Returns menu items for the named menu slug. Falls back to empty array. */
    function nav(string $menuSlug = 'main-nav'): array
    {
        static $cache = [];
        if (isset($cache[$menuSlug])) return $cache[$menuSlug];
        try {
            $pdo  = app(\VeloCMS\Core\Database::class)->core();
            $stmt = $pdo->prepare(
                'SELECT mi.label, mi.url FROM menu_items mi
                 JOIN menus m ON m.id = mi.menu_id
                 WHERE m.slug = ? AND mi.parent_id IS NULL
                 ORDER BY mi.sort_order ASC'
            );
            $stmt->execute([$menuSlug]);
            $cache[$menuSlug] = $stmt->fetchAll(\PDO::FETCH_ASSOC);
        } catch (\Throwable) {
            $cache[$menuSlug] = [];
        }
        return $cache[$menuSlug];
    }
}

if (!function_exists('excerpt')) {
    /** Strip HTML and truncate to $length characters, appending ellipsis. */
    function excerpt(string $html, int $length = 200): string
    {
        $text = strip_tags($html);
        $text = preg_replace('/\s+/', ' ', trim($text)) ?? $text;
        return mb_strlen($text) > $length
            ? mb_substr($text, 0, $length) . '…'
            : $text;
    }
}
if (!function_exists('slug')) {
    function slug(string $text): string
    {
        $text = mb_strtolower($text);
        $text = preg_replace('/[^a-z0-9\s-]/', '', $text) ?? $text;
        return trim(preg_replace('/[\s-]+/', '-', $text) ?? $text, '-');
    }
}

if (!function_exists('date_fmt')) {
    function date_fmt(mixed $dateStr, string $format = 'F j, Y'): string
    {
        if ($dateStr === null || $dateStr === '') {
            return '';
        }
        $dt = \DateTime::createFromFormat('Y-m-d H:i:s', (string)$dateStr)
           ?: \DateTime::createFromFormat('Y-m-d', (string)$dateStr);
        return $dt ? $dt->format($format) : '';
    }
}

<?php declare(strict_types=1);
namespace VeloCMS\Admin;

use VeloCMS\Core\Application;
use VeloCMS\Core\Auth;
use VeloCMS\Core\Response;
use VeloCMS\Core\View;

abstract class BaseController
{
    public function __construct(protected readonly Application $app) {}

    protected function auth(): Auth
    {
        return $this->app->make(Auth::class);
    }

    /** Render an admin view file (no layout). */
    protected function view(string $template, array $data = []): string
    {
        $view = new View($this->app->basePath('admin/views'));
        return $view->with($data)->render($template);
    }

    /** Render a view wrapped in the main admin layout. */
    protected function layout(string $template, array $data = []): Response
    {
        $content = $this->view($template, $data);
        $html    = $this->view('layout.php', array_merge($data, ['content' => $content]));
        return (new Response())->html($html);
    }

    protected function redirect(string $url, int $status = 302): Response
    {
        return (new Response())->redirect($url, $status);
    }
}

// ── API Keys ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

$router->post('/admin/api/keys',              [ApiKeyAdminController::class, 'store']);

use VeloCMS\Modules\Settings\Admin\SettingsAdminController;

$router->get('/admin/booking',                          [BookingAdminController::class, 'index']);

$router->post('/admin/booking/create',                  [BookingAdminController::class, 'createStore']);
$router->get('/admin/booking/settings',                 [BookingAdminController::class, 'settingsForm']);
$router->post('/admin/booking/settings',                [BookingAdminController::class, 'saveSettings']);

// ── Booking — Resources (must be before /{id} wildcard) ─────────────────────
$router->get('/admin/booking/resources',                        [ResourceAdminController::class, 'index']);
$router->get('/admin/booking/resources/create',                 [ResourceAdminController::class, 'create']);
$router->post('/admin/booking/resources',                       [ResourceAdminController::class, 'store']);
$router->get('/admin/booking/resources/{id}/edit',              [ResourceAdminController::class, 'edit']);
$router->post('/admin/booking/resources/{id}',                  [ResourceAdminController::class, 'update']);

$router->post('/admin/booking/resources/{id}/products',         [ResourceAdminController::class, 'storeProduct']);
$router->post('/admin/booking/resources/{id}/products/{prod_id}/delete', [ResourceAdminController::class, 'deleteProduct']);

// ── Booking — Individual booking (wildcard — must be LAST) ──────────────────
$router->get('/admin/booking/{id}/edit',                [BookingAdminController::class, 'editForm']);
$router->post('/admin/booking/{id}/edit',               [BookingAdminController::class, 'editStore']);
$router->post('/admin/booking/{id}/cancel',             [BookingAdminController::class, 'cancel']);

// ── Profile / password change ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
$router->get('/admin/profile',           [ProfileController::class, 'show']);
$router->post('/admin/profile/password', [ProfileController::class, 'changePassword']);

<?php declare(strict_types=1);
namespace VeloCMS\Modules\Settings\Admin;

use VeloCMS\Admin\BaseController;
use VeloCMS\Core\Auth;
use VeloCMS\Core\Database;
use VeloCMS\Core\Request;
use VeloCMS\Core\Response;
use VeloCMS\Modules\Settings\Models\Setting;
use VeloCMS\Modules\Booking\Models\BookingResource;
use VeloCMS\Modules\Booking\Models\BookingPackage;
use VeloCMS\Modules\Booking\Models\BookingProduct;

class SettingsAdminController extends BaseController
{
    private const DEFINITIONS = [
        'site' => [
            ['key' => 'name',        'label' => 'Site Name',    'type' => 'text',     'default' => 'VeloCMS'],
            ['key' => 'tagline',     'label' => 'Tagline',      'type' => 'text',     'default' => ''],
            ['key' => 'admin_email', 'label' => 'Admin Email',  'type' => 'email',    'default' => ''],
            ['key' => 'timezone',    'label' => 'Timezone',     'type' => 'text',     'default' => 'UTC'],
        ],
        'email' => [
            ['key' => 'smtp_host',      'label' => 'SMTP Host',      'type' => 'text',     'default' => ''],
            ['key' => 'smtp_port',      'label' => 'SMTP Port',      'type' => 'number',   'default' => '587'],
            ['key' => 'smtp_user',      'label' => 'SMTP Username',  'type' => 'text',     'default' => ''],
            ['key' => 'smtp_pass',      'label' => 'SMTP Password',  'type' => 'password', 'default' => ''],
            ['key' => 'smtp_from',      'label' => 'From Address',   'type' => 'email',    'default' => ''],
            ['key' => 'smtp_from_name', 'label' => 'From Name',      'type' => 'text',     'default' => 'VeloCMS'],
            ['key' => 'smtp_secure',    'label' => 'Encryption',     'type' => 'select',   'default' => 'tls',
             'options' => ['tls' => 'TLS (STARTTLS)', 'ssl' => 'SSL/TLS', '' => 'None']],
        ],
    ];

    public function index(Request $request, array $params): Response
    {
        $auth = $this->auth();
        $auth->requireLogin();

        $pdo     = $this->app->make(Database::class)->core();
        $model   = new Setting($pdo);
        $current = $model->all();

        // Load booking resources + packages + products for inline management
        $bkResources = [];
        $bkPackages  = [];
        $bkProducts  = [];
        try {
            $bkResources = (new BookingResource($pdo))->all();
            $pkg = new BookingPackage($pdo);
            $prd = new BookingProduct($pdo);
            foreach ($bkResources as $res) {
                $rid = $res['id'];
                foreach ($pkg->allForResource($rid) as $p) $bkPackages[] = $p + ['resource_name' => $res['name']];
                foreach ($prd->allForResource($rid) as $p) $bkProducts[] = $p + ['resource_name' => $res['name']];
            }
        } catch (\Throwable $e) { /* booking tables may not exist yet */ }

        return $this->layout('settings/index.php', [
            'user'        => $auth->user(),
            'page'        => 'settings',
            'definitions' => self::DEFINITIONS,
            'current'     => $current,
            'csrf'        => $auth->csrfToken(),
            'flash'       => $_SESSION['_flash'] ?? null,
            'bkResources' => $bkResources,
            'bkPackages'  => $bkPackages,
            'bkProducts'  => $bkProducts,
        ]);
    }

    public function update(Request $request, array $params): Response
    {
        $auth   = $this->auth();
        $auth->requireLogin();
        $isAjax = $request->header('X-Requested-With') === 'XMLHttpRequest';

        if (!$auth->verifyCsrf()) {
            if ($isAjax) return (new Response())->json(['ok' => false, 'error' => 'csrf']);
            return $this->redirect('/admin/settings');
        }

        $module = $params['module'] ?? '';
        $pdo    = $this->app->make(Database::class)->core();
        $model  = new Setting($pdo);

        if ($module === 'booking') {
            // Scalar settings
            $scalarKeys = [
                'enabled', 'guest_booking', 'require_confirmation',
                'payment_mode', 'deposit_percent',
                'stripe_enabled', 'stripe_pk', 'stripe_sk',
                'notification_email',
                'customer_email_subject', 'customer_email_body',
                'owner_email_subject', 'owner_email_body',
                'cancel_email_subject', 'cancel_email_body',
                'modify_email_subject', 'modify_email_body',
                'slot_duration_mins', 'timezone', 'calendar_sync',
                'section_general_open', 'section_hours_open',
                'section_payment_open', 'section_email_open', 'section_integrations_open',
                'daily_checkin_time', 'daily_checkout_time',
            ];
            foreach ($scalarKeys as $key) {
                $model->set('booking', $key, (string) $request->post($key, ''));
            }

            // Booking types from individual toggles
            $types = [];
            if ($request->post('bktype_hourly',  '0') === '1') $types[] = 'hourly';
            if ($request->post('bktype_package', '0') === '1') $types[] = 'package';
            if ($request->post('bktype_product', '0') === '1') $types[] = 'product';
            if ($request->post('bktype_daily',   '0') === '1') $types[] = 'daily';
            $model->set('booking', 'booking_types', json_encode($types));

            // Per-day business hours
            $bhConfig = [];
            $legacyDays = [];
            for ($d = 1; $d <= 7; $d++) {
                $enabled = $request->post('bh_' . $d . '_enabled', '0') === '1';
                $start   = $request->post('bh_' . $d . '_start', '09:00');
                $end     = $request->post('bh_' . $d . '_end',   '21:00');
                $bhConfig[$d] = ['enabled' => $enabled, 'start' => $start, 'end' => $end];
                if ($enabled) $legacyDays[] = $d;
            }
            $model->set('booking', 'business_days_config', json_encode($bhConfig));
            $model->set('booking', 'business_days', json_encode($legacyDays));
            // Derive global start/end from first enabled day for legacy slot generator
            $firstDay = null;
            for ($d = 1; $d <= 7; $d++) {
                if ($bhConfig[$d]['enabled']) { $firstDay = $bhConfig[$d]; break; }
            }
            if ($firstDay) {
                $model->set('booking', 'business_hours_start', $firstDay['start']);
                $model->set('booking', 'business_hours_end',   $firstDay['end']);
            }

            if ($isAjax) return (new Response())->json(['ok' => true]);
            $_SESSION['_flash'] = 'Booking settings saved.';
            return $this->redirect('/admin/settings?tab=booking');
        }

        if (!isset(self::DEFINITIONS[$module])) {
            if ($isAjax) return (new Response())->json(['ok' => false, 'error' => 'module']);
            return $this->redirect('/admin/settings');
        }

        foreach (self::DEFINITIONS[$module] as $field) {
            $value = $request->post($field['key'], $field['default']);
            $model->set($module, $field['key'], (string) $value);
        }

        if ($isAjax) return (new Response())->json(['ok' => true]);
        $_SESSION['_flash'] = ucfirst($module) . ' settings saved.';
        return $this->redirect('/admin/settings');
    }
}
